title: Static Delivery, HTTP Representations, URLs, Bases, and Origins · Runtime Lens: Http Evidence
summary: Pages work from a local file but break below a project subpath, directories redirect unexpectedly, fragments point nowhere, and the team confuses filesystem paths with URLs. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http evidence increment in versioned URL and delivery contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case prac…
objectives: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.
Define clean URLs, directory indexes, canonical output files, redirects, not-found behavior, and host-specific limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Http Evidence · Theory Http Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 7720 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-http-url-model-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_http_url_model_2(requests: int = 7, mapped_routes: int = 7, base_path: str = "/handbook/", broken_fragments: int = 0, media_types_valid: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1:
        return False, mapped_routes, "request-shape"
    if mapped_routes != requests:
        return False, mapped_routes, "route-mapping"
    if not base_path.startswith("/") or not base_path.endswith("/"):
        return False, mapped_routes, "base-path"
    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"

# Evidence: ssg-http-evidence-inhj1k

        "source4072",
        "parsek1jhni",
        "routeinhj1k4072",

    if broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid:
        return False, broken_fragments, "delivery-contract"
    assert mapped_routes == requests
    return True, mapped_routes, "resolved-static-delivery"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Http Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6151 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Http Evidence model · Theory Http Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Http Evidence model · Theory Http Evidence, keep Http Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Http Evidence model · Theory Http Evidence, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Http Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Http Evidence model · Theory Http Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 6151 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile static requests, base-path URL resolution, routes, fragments, and representation metadata; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify media type, character encoding, caching, compression, conditional delivery, range needs, and error behavior on the target host.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Http Evidence model · Theory Http Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Correct HTML bytes guarantee correct delivery semantics.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-http-evidence-inhj1k.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-http-evidence-inhj1k(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_http_evidence_inhj1k\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?requests < 1 or mapped_routes < 1)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?mapped_routes != requests)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not base_path\.startswith\("/"\) or not base_path\.endswith\("/"\))(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?broken_fragments > 0 or not media_types_valid)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
